# Phase 6 Requirements Checklist: Thin Bolt-v3 Live Canary Path

**Purpose**: Validate Phase 6 submit-admission requirements quality before implementation planning and review.
**Created**: 2026-05-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are Phase 6 boundaries limited to submit admission, with Phase 7 no-submit readiness and Phase 8 tiny-capital canary explicitly excluded? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Tasks §Phase 6, Tasks §Phase 7, Tasks §Phase 8]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all required fail-closed inputs documented: unarmed gate report, exhausted order count, over-cap notional, missing decision evidence, evidence persistence failure, and double-arm/stale-arm? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, FR-009, FR-010, Contract §Fail-closed Conditions]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for how `SubmitAdmissionState` is created, armed, and made available to all strategy submit paths? [Gap, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements documented for entry, exit, and replace-submit candidates consuming the same budget, with plain cancel explicitly excluded? [Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the spec define whether admission budget is consumed only after decision evidence persists and before NT submit? [Completeness, FR-010, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements defined for preserving NT ownership of order lifecycle, fills, cancel behavior, cache, adapter behavior, and reconciliation during Phase 6? [Completeness, FR-011]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "before every live submit" defined as the exact order `decision evidence write -> submit admission -> NT submit_order`? [Clarity, FR-009, FR-010, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK008 Is `max_live_order_count` semantics clear as admitted submit attempts, accepted NT orders, or venue-accepted orders? [Ambiguity, FR-009, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK009 Is `max_notional_per_order` defined with the strategy-owned notional formula for binary-oracle orders? [Ambiguity, FR-009, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "missing decision evidence" distinguished between missing writer at construction and persistence failure at submit time? [Clarity, FR-010, Tasks §Phase 5, Tasks §Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "validated `BoltV3LiveCanaryGateReport`" tied to the current gate output fields and not to stale branch structures? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §BoltV3LiveCanaryGateReport]
- [ ] CHK012 Are double-arm, re-arm, and stale-arm terms defined with observable acceptance or rejection criteria? [Ambiguity, Gap]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Do Phase 6 requirements align with the contract order that startup gate validation happens before runner entry and submit admission happens before NT submit? [Consistency, Contract §Gate Order, Plan §Eight-slice Plan]
- [ ] CHK014 Are task descriptions consistent with FR-010 by saying evidence failure rejects before NT submit without consuming admission budget? [Consistency, FR-010, Tasks §Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK015 Are count-cap and notional-cap requirements consistent between spec scenarios, data model rules, contracts, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState, Contract §Fail-closed Conditions, Tasks §Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK016 Are out-of-scope statements consistent across plan, tasks, and contract documents for Phase 7/8 work? [Consistency, Plan §Eight-slice Plan, Tasks §Out Of Scope For MVP, Contract §Live Proof Requirements]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can the missing-gate-report requirement be objectively evaluated without relying on branch-specific implementation details? [Measurability, FR-009, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK018 Can the count-cap requirement be objectively evaluated for first allowed submit and second rejected submit? [Measurability, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Can the notional-cap requirement be objectively evaluated at the admission boundary before NT submit? [Measurability, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK020 Can the evidence-ordering requirement be objectively evaluated as evidence persistence before admission budget consumption? [Measurability, FR-010, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK021 Are success criteria specific enough to distinguish admission rejection from NT risk-engine rejection? [Acceptance Criteria, SC-004, FR-009]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK022 Are primary-flow requirements documented for a valid gate report, valid evidence write, within-cap order, and subsequent NT submit? [Coverage, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK023 Are exception-flow requirements documented for missing gate report, over notional cap, exhausted count, missing evidence, and evidence persistence failure? [Coverage, Contract §Fail-closed Conditions]
- [ ] CHK024 Are recovery or reset requirements documented for process restart, new `LiveNode` build, or admission state replacement? [Gap, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK025 Are concurrent submit candidates addressed or intentionally excluded from Phase 6 requirements? [Gap, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Are dependencies on Phase 3-5 current main surfaces documented, including registered strategy context and decision-evidence writer availability? [Dependency, Tasks §Phase 3, Tasks §Phase 5]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements clear that stale PR #316/#317 branches are evidence inputs only, not accepted implementation scope? [Assumption, Plan §Current Evidence, Tasks §Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the gate-report source documented as current `check_bolt_v3_live_canary_gate` output rather than duplicated config parsing? [Dependency, Data Model §BoltV3LiveCanaryGateReport, Contract §Gate Order]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK029 Is constant-time in-memory admission checking specified without adding a new hot-path framework or Bolt-owned runtime machinery? [Non-Functional, Plan §Technical Context, FR-011]
- [ ] CHK030 Are no-hardcode and no-dual-path constraints traceable to concrete Phase 6 requirement language? [Non-Functional, FR-004, FR-005, FR-006]
- [ ] CHK031 Are testability requirements specified through public behavior and source fences without requiring private implementation coupling? [Testability, Tasks §Phase 6, SC-004]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK032 Is there any conflict between consuming admission budget before NT submit and possible NT submit failure handling? [Ambiguity, FR-009, Contract §Gate Order]
- [ ] CHK033 Is there any conflict between startup gate validation and submit-time admission state arming order? [Ambiguity, Contract §Gate Order, Data Model §SubmitAdmissionState]
- [ ] CHK034 Are definitions for "live order candidate", "admitted order", and "submit attempt" explicit enough to prevent alternate interpretations? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 2]
